default.overview: Use clean pruning, balanced nutrition, and preventive fungicide rotation.

timeline.action: Isolate infected plants and remove heavily damaged leaves.
Spray broad-spectrum bio-fungicide in early morning.
Improve airflow and reduce overhead watering.
Apply micronutrient foliar feed to reduce stress.
Re-check lesions and remove newly infected foliage.
Apply second preventive spray with alternate mode of action.
Monitor new growth; continue hygiene and weekly scouting.

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.overview: Bacterial infection favored by warm, wet foliage.

timeline.action: Remove infected leaves and avoid handling wet plants.
Apply copper-based bactericide at label dosage.
Switch to drip irrigation; stop overhead watering.
Disinfect tools with 1% bleach or alcohol.
Scout lower canopy for new spotting.
Repeat copper spray if disease pressure remains high.
Mulch soil to reduce splash spread and continue monitoring.

Tomato___Early_blight.overview: Fungal disease with concentric ring lesions on older leaves.

timeline.action: Remove severely infected lower leaves.
Apply chlorothalonil or mancozeb fungicide.
Stake plants and prune for airflow.
Apply compost tea or bio-control as supportive measure.
Inspect for spread near stem base.
Rotate fungicide chemistry and re-spray if needed.
Maintain soil moisture consistency and continue scouting.

Tomato___Late_blight.overview: Aggressive oomycete disease; acts fast under cool humid weather.

timeline.action: Immediately isolate infected plants and remove major lesions.
Apply systemic + contact blight fungicide mix.
Destroy severely infected plants away from field.
Improve airflow and avoid leaf wetness.
Reassess neighboring plants for water-soaked lesions.
Repeat spray with alternate chemistry.
Continue strict sanitation and daily monitoring.

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.overview: Fungal leaf mold common in humid protected cultivation.

timeline.action: Remove infected lower leaves and reduce humidity.
Apply suitable fungicide targeting leaf mold.
Ventilate greenhouse and increase row spacing airflow.
Water at soil level only.
Check undersides of leaves for olive growth.
Repeat fungicide if symptoms persist.
Maintain RH below 85% where possible.

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.overview: Fungal leaf spot producing many small lesions.

timeline.action: Remove most infected leaves; bag and discard.
Apply protective fungicide spray.
Add mulch to reduce soil splash.
Avoid working in crop when foliage is wet.
Scout lower canopy for fresh spots.
Repeat spray in 5-7 day interval if required.
Support plant vigor with balanced feeding.

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.overview: Mite infestation causing stippling and webbing.

timeline.action: Wash leaf undersides with clean water jet.
Apply neem oil or miticide as per label.
Increase ambient humidity where feasible.
Release predatory mites in controlled environments.
Inspect webbing intensity and hot spots.
Second miticide spray with different mode of action.
Continue weekly scouting and edge plant checks.

Tomato___Target_Spot.overview: Fungal disease with circular target-like lesions.

timeline.action: Prune infected leaves and improve air movement.
Spray broad-spectrum fungicide.
Apply mulch and avoid splash irrigation.
Check new growth for lesion expansion.
Remove fallen debris near root zone.
Repeat preventive spray if humidity persists.
Sustain sanitation and regular scouting.
Rogue heavily infected plants immediately.
Install yellow sticky traps for whitefly management.
Apply recommended insecticide targeting vectors.
Use reflective mulch where possible.
Inspect neighboring plants for curling symptoms.
Follow-up vector control spray rotation.
Maintain weed control around crop border.
Remove infected plants and sanitize tools.
Disinfect hands and equipment frequently.
Restrict movement from infected to healthy rows.
Control chewing insects that aid spread.
Monitor mosaic patterns and deformed leaves.
Continue strict hygiene protocol.
Plan resistant cultivar usage for next cycle.

Tomato___healthy.overview: Plant appears healthy. Continue preventive care.

timeline.action: Maintain regular irrigation schedule.
Inspect underside of leaves for pests.
Apply balanced nutrition if needed.
Remove yellowing old leaves only.
Keep field weed-free and clean.
Monitor canopy airflow.
Repeat weekly preventive scouting.

Potato___Early_blight.overview: Alternaria infection causing dark concentric lesions.

timeline.action: Remove infected foliage where practical.
Apply protectant fungicide.
Ensure uniform irrigation and reduce stress.
Inspect field hot spots and low airflow zones.
Add potassium-rich feed for resilience.
Repeat spray with alternate active ingredient.
Continue crop sanitation and monitor spread.

Potato___Late_blight.overview: Rapidly spreading blight under cool wet conditions.

timeline.action: Remove and destroy heavily infected plants.
Apply anti-oomycete fungicide promptly.
Reduce irrigation frequency and avoid wet foliage.
Inspect adjacent rows closely.
Apply second protective spray if needed.
Dispose of infected residues safely.
Continue daily field scouting during risk period.

Potato___healthy.overview: Potato foliage is healthy. Maintain crop hygiene.

timeline.action: Irrigate consistently and avoid waterlogging.
Scout for aphids and beetles.
Apply preventive biocontrol if preferred.
Remove volunteer plants around field edges.
Check for early lesions after rains.
Maintain balanced fertilization.
Document weekly health observation.

Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.overview: Bacterial spot causing dark water-soaked lesions.

timeline.action: Remove infected leaves and reduce splash contact.
Apply copper bactericide mix.
Shift to drip irrigation pattern.
Sanitize pruning tools between plants.
Scout young leaves for fresh spots.
Repeat copper spray if weather remains humid.
Maintain mulch cover and hygiene.
Continue irrigation and nutrient schedule.
Inspect canopy for minor insect activity.
Keep field clean and weed-free.
Improve airflow by light pruning if dense.
Check leaf undersides for hidden pests.
Apply preventive foliar micronutrients.
Log plant health and continue monitoring.
